test_series_name: WTO Agreement on Customs Valuation - 12 Main Tests
notes: These tests are based on the uploaded WTO Agreement on Customs Valuation PDF.
The sequence follows a progression from foundational recall to broader application and mixed-topic reasoning.
Time limits increase toward later tests.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid consecutive repetition of the same answer letter.

tests.title: WTO Agreement on Customs Valuation - Main Test 1
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why does Article 20 provide special and differential treatment?

options.A: Because all Members must be permanently exempt from valuation rules
options.B: Because developed countries refused transaction value
options.C: Because developing country Members might face difficulties implementing all provisions when the WTO was created
options.D: Because valuation rules apply only to agriculture

questions.explanation: Article 20 addresses developing-country implementation difficulties.

questions.question: What does Part III of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Currency conversion only
options.B: Publication only
options.C: Definitions only
options.D: Special and differential treatment in Article 20

questions.explanation: Part III is the S&D treatment section.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does the WTO Valuation Committee mainly handle?

options.A: Issuing HS codes
options.B: Policy issues and implementation issues
options.C: Day-to-day cargo release at ports
options.D: Setting exchange rates for all Members

questions.explanation: The WTO Committee focuses on policy and implementation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Must the order of valuation methods be followed?

options.A: Yes, the order must be followed
options.B: No, the importer may skip directly to Method 6
options.C: Only Methods 1 to 3 have to be followed
options.D: No, customs can choose any method freely

questions.explanation: The Agreement requires sequential use of the methods.

questions.question: Can transaction value be rejected if customs has unresolved reasonable doubts after following the proper process?

options.A: Only if the goods came by sea
options.B: No, transaction value can never be rejected
options.C: No, if the importer is related to the seller
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: After the required procedure, transaction value may be rejected if doubts remain unresolved.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 5?

options.A: Fall-back method
options.B: Computed value
options.C: Deductive value
options.D: Transaction value of similar goods

questions.explanation: Method 5 is computed value.

questions.question: The buyer supplied molds free of charge to the foreign producer for use in manufacturing the imported goods, and that value is not already in the invoice price. Which concept is most relevant?

options.A: A domestic price benchmark
options.B: A prohibited minimum value
options.C: An assist that may need to be added under Article 8 if objectively quantifiable
options.D: A post-importation cost that must be excluded

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production inputs can be assists under Article 8.

questions.question: Why are exporters concerned about unfair customs valuation?

options.A: Because inflated customs values can undermine negotiated tariff reductions and market access benefits
options.B: Because it prevents transport by sea
options.C: Because it removes all need for certificates of origin
options.D: Because it eliminates all customs formalities

questions.explanation: Inflated customs values can nullify negotiated market-access gains.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why does the relationship between buyer and seller matter in customs valuation?

options.A: Because related parties are always forbidden to trade
options.B: Because customs always ignores related-party sales
options.C: Because the relationship may or may not have influenced the price
options.D: Because relationship alone makes the value invalid

questions.explanation: The central question is whether the price was influenced.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article deals with publication?

options.A: Article 12
options.B: Article 16
options.C: Article 10
options.D: Article 13

questions.explanation: Publication is governed by Article 12.

questions.question: Customs cannot use Methods 1 to 5. An officer proposes inventing a fixed benchmark value unrelated to the Agreement. Is that allowed under Method 6?

options.A: Yes; if the benchmark is higher than invoice price
options.B: No; even the fall-back method cannot use arbitrary or fictitious values
options.C: Yes; Method 6 allows any value customs prefers
options.D: Yes; if the importer is a related party

questions.explanation: The fall-back method remains constrained by the Agreement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? How must customs communicate its final decision where it rejects the declared value?

options.A: It may do so verbally only
options.B: It need not explain the rejection at all
options.C: It may notify only the carrier
options.D: It must inform the importer in writing

questions.explanation: A written communication is required.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs value be based on the price of goods for export to a country other than the country of importation?

options.A: Yes, whenever the importer requests it
options.B: Yes, if that country’s price is higher
options.C: No, that is prohibited
options.D: Yes, if the goods are similar

questions.explanation: Third-country export prices are prohibited.

questions.question: Why are exporters concerned about unfair customs valuation?

options.A: Because it prevents transport by sea
options.B: Because inflated customs values can undermine negotiated tariff reductions and market access benefits
options.C: Because it removes all need for certificates of origin
options.D: Because it eliminates all customs formalities

questions.explanation: Inflated customs values can nullify negotiated market-access gains.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Must the order of valuation methods be followed?

options.A: No, customs can choose any method freely
options.B: Only Methods 1 to 3 have to be followed
options.C: No, the importer may skip directly to Method 6
options.D: Yes, the order must be followed

questions.explanation: The Agreement requires sequential use of the methods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Part IV of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Final provisions in Articles 21 to 24
options.B: The six valuation methods
options.C: The Technical Committee’s day-to-day rulings
options.D: Importer rights only

questions.explanation: Part IV contains the final provisions.

questions.question: What should customs consider second when examining the circumstances of sale?

options.A: How the goods were packaged
options.B: How many containers were used
options.C: How the price in question was derived
options.D: Whether the importer used a letter of credit

questions.explanation: Customs also examines price derivation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which article deals with currency conversion?

options.A: Article 9
options.B: Article 13
options.C: Article 11
options.D: Article 10

questions.explanation: Article 9 addresses currency conversion.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs value be based on the domestic market price of the country of exportation?

options.A: Yes, if those prices are publicly available
options.B: No, that is prohibited
options.C: Yes, when transport costs are unclear
options.D: Yes, if the goods are discounted

questions.explanation: Export-country domestic prices are prohibited.

questions.question: Can buyer and seller be related if both are controlled by a third person?

options.A: No
options.B: Only if they trade identical goods
options.C: Only if both are in the same country
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Common third-party control is a basis of relatedness.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What kind of transfer must the sale involve for it to qualify under transaction value?

options.A: An international transfer of goods
options.B: Only a temporary stock movement
options.C: A purely domestic sale
options.D: A movement between two warehouses in one country

questions.explanation: The sale must be linked to export to the country of importation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 2?

options.A: Transaction value of similar goods
options.B: Computed value
options.C: Transaction value
options.D: Transaction value of identical goods

questions.explanation: Method 2 is identical goods.

questions.question: What was one major weakness of the Tokyo Round Valuation Code before the Uruguay Round?

options.A: It prohibited transaction value
options.B: It applied only to sea freight imports
options.C: Less than one-third of the GATT contracting parties had signed it
options.D: It had no valuation methods at all

questions.explanation: Limited participation was a key weakness.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are royalties and licence fees among the possible Article 8 additions?

options.A: Only for domestic sales
options.B: Yes
options.C: No
options.D: Only if there is no invoice

questions.explanation: Royalties and licence fees can be additions where conditions are met.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Part I of the Customs Valuation Agreement cover?

options.A: Rules on customs valuation in Articles 1 to 17
options.B: Special and differential treatment only
options.C: Final provisions only
options.D: Administration and dispute settlement only

questions.explanation: Part I contains the valuation rules.

questions.question: What did GATT 1947 say customs value should not be based on?

options.A: Objective and quantifiable data
options.B: A sale for export to the country of importation
options.C: The transaction value of imported goods
options.D: The value of merchandise of national origin or arbitrary or fictitious values

questions.explanation: GATT 1947 prohibited customs value based on domestic merchandise or arbitrary/fictitious values.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which articles contain the valuation methods?

options.A: Annex III only
options.B: Articles 9 to 12
options.C: Articles 1 to 8
options.D: Articles 18 to 24

questions.explanation: The six methods are found in Articles 1 through 8.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article deals with publication?

options.A: Article 16
options.B: Article 12
options.C: Article 13
options.D: Article 10

questions.explanation: Publication is governed by Article 12.

questions.question: Can transaction value be used in related-party sales under Article 1.1(d)?

options.A: Only if the seller is in the exporting country
options.B: Only if the importer waives appeal rights
options.C: Yes, but only if the relationship did not influence the price
options.D: No, never

questions.explanation: Related-party sales may still qualify if the price was not influenced.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which GATT article deals with fees and formalities connected with importation and exportation?

options.A: Article V
options.B: Article VIII
options.C: Article IX
options.D: Article VII

questions.explanation: Article VIII covers fees and formalities.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are minimum customs values allowed under the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: Yes, if based on domestic law
options.B: Yes, if announced in advance
options.C: Yes, for all developing countries
options.D: No, minimum customs values are prohibited

questions.explanation: Minimum values are prohibited.

questions.question: Which GATT article deals with publication and administration of trade regulations?

options.A: Article X
options.B: Article VII
options.C: Article V
options.D: Article VIII

questions.explanation: Article X covers publication and administration of trade regulations.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Part II of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Valuation methods only
options.B: Administration, consultations, and dispute settlement in Articles 18 and 19
options.C: Annexes only
options.D: Special and differential treatment only

questions.explanation: Part II deals with institutional and dispute matters.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why is customs value important when duty is ad valorem?

options.A: Because ad valorem duties ignore value entirely
options.B: Because customs value replaces tariff rates
options.C: Because the customs value is the base on which the percentage duty is calculated
options.D: Because customs value matters only for statistics

questions.explanation: Ad valorem duty is calculated as a percentage of customs value.

questions.question: What may customs do if it has reason to doubt the truth or accuracy of the declared value?

options.A: It may ask the importer for additional information or documentation
options.B: It must jump directly to computed value without explanation
options.C: It must immediately impose minimum values
options.D: It must automatically accept the value

questions.explanation: Customs may request additional information when doubts arise.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What method comes after deductive value?

options.A: Transaction value of identical goods
options.B: Domestic market price
options.C: The fall-back method
options.D: Computed value under Article 6

questions.explanation: Method 5 is computed value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the key question when using test values in related-party valuation?

options.A: Whether the test values closely approximate the import price
options.B: Whether the test value is arbitrary
options.C: Whether the test value comes from domestic sales
options.D: Whether the test value is always higher than the import price

questions.explanation: The comparison is based on close approximation.

questions.question: What broad problem do customs valuation rules try to prevent?

options.A: Use of transaction value as a primary method
options.B: Publication of customs legislation
options.C: Arbitrary, fictitious, or inappropriate customs values
options.D: Legitimate market discounts

questions.explanation: The Agreement seeks to prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What was adopted in November 1979 regarding customs valuation?

options.A: The Nairobi Ministerial Decision on Trade Facilitation
options.B: The Harmonized System Convention
options.C: The WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement
options.D: A Protocol to the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII

questions.explanation: The Tokyo Round produced the 1979 protocol.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 3?

options.A: Deductive value
options.B: Transaction value of similar goods
options.C: Transaction value of identical goods
options.D: Computed value

questions.explanation: Method 3 is similar goods.

questions.question: Why are exporters concerned about unfair customs valuation?

options.A: Because it removes all need for certificates of origin
options.B: Because it eliminates all customs formalities
options.C: Because inflated customs values can undermine negotiated tariff reductions and market access benefits
options.D: Because it prevents transport by sea

questions.explanation: Inflated customs values can nullify negotiated market-access gains.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the purpose of notifying customs valuation legislation to the Committee?

options.A: To provide the title, details, and relevant text of a Member’s national customs valuation legislation
options.B: To set a Member’s customs duties
options.C: To substitute for domestic publication
options.D: To approve all import transactions individually

questions.explanation: Members notify legislation for transparency and committee review.

questions.question: An importer disagrees with a customs valuation decision and wants an official avenue to challenge it. Which safeguard in the Agreement is most directly relevant?

options.A: The rule on carrier media decisions
options.B: The right of appeal under Article 11
options.C: The rule on currency conversion under Article 9
options.D: The annex on developing countries

questions.explanation: Article 11 provides the appeal right.

questions.question: Under Article 15, are members of the same family considered related parties?

options.A: Only if they own a bank together
options.B: No
options.C: Only if they are customs agents
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Family members are included among related persons.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What four core elements make up transaction value under Article 1.1?

options.A: Any customs-selected benchmark plus margins
options.B: Domestic selling price, taxes, profit margin, and penalties
options.C: Price actually paid or payable, for the goods, when sold for export, plus permitted adjustments
options.D: List price, insurance, profit, and exchange control charges

questions.explanation: These are the core elements of transaction value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can international transport costs be an Article 8 addition?

options.A: Only for domestic shipments
options.B: Only if paid after customs clearance
options.C: No
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Transport costs may be included under the Agreement depending on national option and applicable rules.

questions.question: What did GATT 1947 say customs value should not be based on?

options.A: The value of merchandise of national origin or arbitrary or fictitious values
options.B: Objective and quantifiable data
options.C: The transaction value of imported goods
options.D: A sale for export to the country of importation

questions.explanation: GATT 1947 prohibited customs value based on domestic merchandise or arbitrary/fictitious values.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can buyer and seller be related if both are controlled by a third person?

options.A: Only if both are in the same country
options.B: Yes
options.C: No
options.D: Only if they trade identical goods

questions.explanation: Common third-party control is a basis of relatedness.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article deals with publication?

options.A: Article 13
options.B: Article 16
options.C: Article 12
options.D: Article 10

questions.explanation: Publication is governed by Article 12.

questions.question: What four core elements make up transaction value under Article 1.1?

options.A: Any customs-selected benchmark plus margins
options.B: List price, insurance, profit, and exchange control charges
options.C: Domestic selling price, taxes, profit margin, and penalties
options.D: Price actually paid or payable, for the goods, when sold for export, plus permitted adjustments

questions.explanation: These are the core elements of transaction value.

tests.title: WTO Agreement on Customs Valuation - Main Test 2
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Can customs value be based on cost of production other than computed value?

options.A: Yes, whenever customs suspects undervaluation
options.B: Yes, if domestic law prefers it
options.C: No, that is prohibited
options.D: Yes, if the seller is related

questions.explanation: Only the computed value method can use production-cost concepts in the lawful hierarchy.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What must customs do if it doubts the declared value?

options.A: It must suspend all imports indefinitely
options.B: It must notify only the exporter
options.C: It must keep the reasons secret
options.D: It must inform the importer of the reasons for its doubts

questions.explanation: The importer must be informed of the reasons.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country applying to join the WTO still uses official minimum customs values for many imports. What is the likely accession issue?

options.A: Acceding Members are exempt from valuation disciplines permanently
options.B: WTO Members generally require elimination of minimum values and full implementation of the Agreement from accession
options.C: Reference prices replace the need for transaction value in accession talks
options.D: Minimum values are encouraged during accession

questions.explanation: Minimum values are a common accession problem because they are prohibited.

questions.question: Can customs value be based on cost of production other than computed value?

options.A: No, that is prohibited
options.B: Yes, whenever customs suspects undervaluation
options.C: Yes, if domestic law prefers it
options.D: Yes, if the seller is related

questions.explanation: Only the computed value method can use production-cost concepts in the lawful hierarchy.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are buyer and seller related if one is the employer of the other?

options.A: No
options.B: Only in computed value cases
options.C: Yes
options.D: Only if there is a discount

questions.explanation: Employer-employee relation is one category.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article deals with responsibilities of the WTO and WCO Secretariats?

options.A: Article 22
options.B: Article 23
options.C: Article 21
options.D: Article 24

questions.explanation: Article 24 allocates secretariat responsibilities.

questions.question: What procedural fairness must customs provide where there are reasonable doubts?

options.A: A requirement that the importer accept customs’ value immediately
options.B: A reasonable opportunity for the importer to respond
options.C: An obligation to waive the duty
options.D: An automatic penalty before review

questions.explanation: The importer must be given a chance to respond.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What was meant by 'actual value' under GATT 1947?

options.A: The price of the imported merchandise, or like merchandise, in ordinary-course sales under fully competitive conditions
options.B: Only the domestic market price in the country of export
options.C: The higher of two alternative values
options.D: Any price customs considered desirable

questions.explanation: GATT 1947 used the concept of actual value in ordinary trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is one proof that the relationship did not influence the price?

options.A: The seller is overseas
options.B: The price is consistent with normal industry practice
options.C: The goods are subject to ad valorem duty
options.D: The price is always the highest in the market

questions.explanation: Normal industry practice can indicate no influence.

questions.question: Are arbitrary and fictitious values allowed under the Agreement?

options.A: No, they are expressly prohibited
options.B: Yes, in emergency situations
options.C: Yes, when customs suspects fraud
options.D: Yes, if identical goods cannot be found

questions.explanation: Arbitrary or fictitious values are not permitted.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Must the order of valuation methods be followed?

options.A: Only Methods 1 to 3 have to be followed
options.B: No, the importer may skip directly to Method 6
options.C: No, customs can choose any method freely
options.D: Yes, the order must be followed

questions.explanation: The Agreement requires sequential use of the methods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why is customs value important when duty is ad valorem?

options.A: Because customs value matters only for statistics
options.B: Because customs value replaces tariff rates
options.C: Because the customs value is the base on which the percentage duty is calculated
options.D: Because ad valorem duties ignore value entirely

questions.explanation: Ad valorem duty is calculated as a percentage of customs value.

questions.question: What is Method 3?

options.A: Computed value
options.B: Transaction value of identical goods
options.C: Deductive value
options.D: Transaction value of similar goods

questions.explanation: Method 3 is similar goods.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What are 'assists' under Article 8 in general terms?

options.A: Certain goods or services supplied by the buyer for use in producing the imported goods, which may need to be added if not already included
options.B: Any payment made by the bank
options.C: All transport costs paid after import
options.D: All domestic services unrelated to production

questions.explanation: Assists are buyer-supplied inputs or services related to production.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 6?

options.A: Minimum value method
options.B: The highest-of-two-values method
options.C: The fall-back method
options.D: The domestic price method

questions.explanation: Method 6 is the fall-back method.

questions.question: An officer says: 'I will use the domestic selling price of similar locally made goods because that will produce a higher customs value.' Which rule does this violate?

options.A: The right of appeal
options.B: The prohibition on using the domestic selling price of domestically produced goods as customs value
options.C: The rule on publication only
options.D: The developing-country transition rule

questions.explanation: Domestic prices of national-origin goods are prohibited valuation bases.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why are rules on customs valuation needed?

options.A: To replace tariff schedules with fixed minimum values
options.B: To guarantee the highest possible customs revenue
options.C: To allow customs to set any values it prefers
options.D: To ensure a fair, uniform, and neutral system for valuing goods for customs purposes and to prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values

questions.explanation: The Agreement aims to ensure fair, uniform, and neutral valuation and prevent arbitrary or fictitious values.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article deals with publication?

options.A: Article 10
options.B: Article 12
options.C: Article 16
options.D: Article 13

questions.explanation: Publication is governed by Article 12.

questions.question: What other instruments form part of the current customs valuation rules besides the Agreement itself?

options.A: Only domestic court judgments
options.B: Only the Harmonized System Explanatory Notes
options.C: Ministerial decisions and Committee decisions, including decisions on reasonable doubts, minimum values and sole agents, and G/VAL/5
options.D: Only tariff schedules

questions.explanation: Current valuation rules include relevant ministerial and committee decisions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is one proof that the relationship did not influence the price?

options.A: The price is consistent with normal industry practice
options.B: The price is always the highest in the market
options.C: The goods are subject to ad valorem duty
options.D: The seller is overseas

questions.explanation: Normal industry practice can indicate no influence.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs value be based on the higher of two alternative values?

options.A: Yes, but only for related parties
options.B: Yes, if customs has doubts
options.C: Yes, because it protects revenue
options.D: No, that is prohibited

questions.explanation: The higher-of-two-values approach is prohibited.

questions.question: How must customs communicate its final decision where it rejects the declared value?

options.A: It must inform the importer in writing
options.B: It may do so verbally only
options.C: It may notify only the carrier
options.D: It need not explain the rejection at all

questions.explanation: A written communication is required.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A shipment is sold for export to the importing country, the invoice shows a lower-than-average price because of a volume discount, and customs has no other evidence of manipulation. What is the best starting method?

options.A: Automatic rejection because the price is low
options.B: Transaction value under Method 1
options.C: Deductive value under Method 4
options.D: Immediate use of minimum values

questions.explanation: A low price or discount alone does not disqualify Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name another type of addition listed in Article 8.

options.A: General company dividends
options.B: Importer’s internal accounting costs
options.C: Containers and packaging
options.D: Domestic retail margin

questions.explanation: Containers and packaging are possible additions.

questions.question: Which article gives the importer the right to a written explanation?

options.A: Article 16
options.B: Article 11
options.C: Article 10
options.D: Article 13

questions.explanation: Article 16 addresses the written explanation right.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are royalties and licence fees among the possible Article 8 additions?

options.A: Only if there is no invoice
options.B: Yes
options.C: Only for domestic sales
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Royalties and licence fees can be additions where conditions are met.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What kind of transfer must the sale involve for it to qualify under transaction value?

options.A: A purely domestic sale
options.B: A movement between two warehouses in one country
options.C: An international transfer of goods
options.D: Only a temporary stock movement

questions.explanation: The sale must be linked to export to the country of importation.

questions.question: An officer says: 'I will use the domestic selling price of similar locally made goods because that will produce a higher customs value.' Which rule does this violate?

options.A: The rule on publication only
options.B: The right of appeal
options.C: The developing-country transition rule
options.D: The prohibition on using the domestic selling price of domestically produced goods as customs value

questions.explanation: Domestic prices of national-origin goods are prohibited valuation bases.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What method comes immediately after transaction value if Method 1 cannot be used?

options.A: Transaction value of identical goods under Article 2
options.B: Deductive value under Article 5
options.C: The fall-back method under Article 7
options.D: Transaction value of similar goods under Article 3

questions.explanation: Method 2 follows Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 5?

options.A: Deductive value
options.B: Computed value
options.C: Fall-back method
options.D: Transaction value of similar goods

questions.explanation: Method 5 is computed value.

questions.question: What is the last method in the hierarchy?

options.A: Domestic selling price
options.B: Computed value
options.C: The fall-back method under Article 7
options.D: Transaction value of similar goods

questions.explanation: Method 6 is the fall-back method.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Part IV of the Agreement cover?

options.A: The Technical Committee’s day-to-day rulings
options.B: Importer rights only
options.C: The six valuation methods
options.D: Final provisions in Articles 21 to 24

questions.explanation: Part IV contains the final provisions.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A shipment is sold for export to the importing country, the invoice shows a lower-than-average price because of a volume discount, and customs has no other evidence of manipulation. What is the best starting method?

options.A: Transaction value under Method 1
options.B: Automatic rejection because the price is low
options.C: Immediate use of minimum values
options.D: Deductive value under Method 4

questions.explanation: A low price or discount alone does not disqualify Method 1.

questions.question: What is the general objective of customs management and regulation under the GATT in relation to valuation?

options.A: To replace WTO rules with domestic price controls
options.B: To ensure customs operations do not unnecessarily hinder international trade or offset concessions exchanged between Members
options.C: To maximize customs barriers wherever possible
options.D: To require all Members to use minimum values

questions.explanation: The GATT framework seeks to avoid customs operations that unnecessarily hinder trade.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What voting stock threshold helps establish relatedness through common ownership or control?

options.A: One percent
options.B: Fifty percent
options.C: Ten percent
options.D: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person

questions.explanation: The Agreement uses the five-percent threshold in this category.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why do notification requirements exist under Article 22.1?

options.A: To remove the need for national legislation
options.B: To replace dispute settlement
options.C: To provide transparency to the WTO Committee on each Member’s implementation of the Agreement
options.D: To set tariff bindings automatically

questions.explanation: Notifications support transparency and review.

questions.question: Which article deals with responsibilities of the WTO and WCO Secretariats?

options.A: Article 23
options.B: Article 24

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What kind of transfer must the sale involve for it to qualify under transaction value?

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs value be based on cost of production other than computed value?

questions.question: Why is customs value important when duty is ad valorem?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Name one type of addition listed in Article 8.

options.A: Domestic taxes in the importing country
options.B: Commissions and brokerage fees
options.C: Interest charges only
options.D: Post-import advertising expenses only

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? When can Article 8 adjustments be added to the price?

questions.question: Are proceeds of subsequent resale among the Article 8 additions?

options.A: Only if the importer is unrelated

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Name one type of addition listed in Article 8.

options.A: Domestic taxes in the importing country
options.B: Interest charges only
options.C: Commissions and brokerage fees
options.D: Post-import advertising expenses only

questions.explanation: Commissions and brokerage can be added when conditions are met.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can buyer and seller be related if both are controlled by a third person?

options.A: Yes
options.B: Only if they trade identical goods

options.D: Only if both are in the same country

questions.question: What is the baseline commitment for acceding WTO Members regarding customs valuation?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What key agreement resulted from the Uruguay Round in 1994 on customs valuation?

options.A: The Agreement on Rules of Origin

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What method comes after deductive value?

options.D: Transaction value of identical goods

questions.question: Are officers or directors of one another’s businesses related parties?

options.B: Only for WTO accession cases

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What kinds of payments are included in the total payment for transaction value purposes?

tests.title: WTO Agreement on Customs Valuation - Main Test 3
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.

Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What are 'assists' under Article 8 in general terms?

options.A: Any payment made by the bank
options.B: Certain goods or services supplied by the buyer for use in producing the imported goods, which may need to be added if not already included
options.C: All transport costs paid after import
options.D: All domestic services unrelated to production

questions.explanation: Assists are buyer-supplied inputs or services related to production.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why does Article 20 provide special and differential treatment?

options.A: Because developing country Members might face difficulties implementing all provisions when the WTO was created
options.B: Because developed countries refused transaction value
options.C: Because all Members must be permanently exempt from valuation rules
options.D: Because valuation rules apply only to agriculture

questions.explanation: Article 20 addresses developing-country implementation difficulties.

questions.question: What does Part I of the Customs Valuation Agreement cover?

options.A: Administration and dispute settlement only
options.B: Special and differential treatment only
options.C: Rules on customs valuation in Articles 1 to 17
options.D: Final provisions only

questions.explanation: Part I contains the valuation rules.

questions.question: Scenario variant: There is no actual sale for export to the country of importation, only a transfer of goods between branches of the same company without sale. What follows?

options.A: Customs must use domestic market price
options.B: Method 1 still applies automatically
options.C: Customs must use minimum values
options.D: Method 1 transaction value cannot be used because there is no sale

questions.explanation: A sale is required for Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What should customs consider second when examining the circumstances of sale?

options.A: How the goods were packaged

options.C: How many containers were used
options.D: Whether the importer used a letter of credit

questions.question: What method comes after transaction value of similar goods?

options.A: Computed value under Article 6
options.B: Fall-back under Article 7
options.C: Deductive value under Article 5
options.D: Transaction value again

questions.explanation: Method 4 is deductive value.

questions.question: A country applying to join the WTO still uses official minimum customs values for many imports. What is the likely accession issue?

options.A: WTO Members generally require elimination of minimum values and full implementation of the Agreement from accession
options.B: Reference prices replace the need for transaction value in accession talks
options.C: Acceding Members are exempt from valuation disciplines permanently
options.D: Minimum values are encouraged during accession

questions.explanation: Minimum values are a common accession problem because they are prohibited.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs cannot use Methods 1 to 5. An officer proposes inventing a fixed benchmark value unrelated to the Agreement. Is that allowed under Method 6?

options.A: Yes; Method 6 allows any value customs prefers
options.B: Yes; if the benchmark is higher than invoice price
options.C: Yes; if the importer is a related party
options.D: No; even the fall-back method cannot use arbitrary or fictitious values

questions.question: What are the two decisions mentioned as requiring notifications relating to interpretation of the Agreement?

options.A: The Decision on Tariff Bindings and the Decision on Transit

options.D: The Decision on SPS Measures and the Decision on Subsidies

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Annex I contain?

options.A: Interpretative Notes
options.B: Developing-country transition rules only
options.C: Only definitions of related parties
options.D: The WTO Committee rules of procedure

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Part II of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Annexes only
options.B: Special and differential treatment only
options.C: Valuation methods only
options.D: Administration, consultations, and dispute settlement in Articles 18 and 19

questions.question: What two committees are established under Article 18?

options.A: Only one committee at the WTO Secretariat

options.C: The SPS Committee and TBT Committee

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What voting stock threshold helps establish relatedness through common ownership or control?

options.A: Fifty percent
options.B: Ten percent
options.C: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person
options.D: One percent

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What must a Member notify if it applies the Decision on the Treatment of Interest Charges?

options.A: The names of all importers using bank finance
options.B: Its annual customs revenue

options.D: The date from which it implemented the decision

questions.question: Can transaction value be used in related-party sales under Article 1.1(d)?

options.A: Yes, but only if the relationship did not influence the price

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the purpose of notifying customs valuation legislation to the Committee?

options.A: To set a Member’s customs duties

options.C: To approve all import transactions individually
options.D: To substitute for domestic publication

questions.question: Customs cannot use Methods 1 to 5. An officer proposes inventing a fixed benchmark value unrelated to the Agreement. Is that allowed under Method 6?

options.A: Yes; if the importer is a related party
options.B: Yes; Method 6 allows any value customs prefers
options.C: No; even the fall-back method cannot use arbitrary or fictitious values
options.D: Yes; if the benchmark is higher than invoice price

questions.explanation: The fall-back method remains constrained by the Agreement.

questions.question: What does 'price actually paid or payable' mean in customs valuation?

options.A: Only the seller’s cost of production
options.B: Whatever amount the buyer agrees to pay the seller to obtain the goods
options.C: Only the amount customs thinks is reasonable
options.D: Only the amount stated in a public price list

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are minimum customs values allowed under the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: Yes, for all developing countries
options.B: Yes, if based on domestic law
options.C: Yes, if announced in advance
options.D: No, minimum customs values are prohibited

questions.explanation: Minimum values are prohibited.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importing country binds tariffs at low ad valorem rates, but customs officers start inflating customs values to raise more duty. Which key problem identified in the Agreement does this illustrate?

options.A: Inflated customs values can nullify tariff concessions and undermine market access
options.B: Article 10 confidentiality breaches
options.C: Transaction value always prevents under-collection only
options.D: The lawful use of minimum values

questions.explanation: The Agreement exists in part to prevent inflated values from defeating tariff concessions.

questions.question: Can buyer and seller be related if both are controlled by a third person?

options.A: No
options.B: Only if both are in the same country
options.C: Yes
options.D: Only if they trade identical goods

questions.explanation: Common third-party control is a basis of relatedness.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can international transport costs be an Article 8 addition?

options.A: Yes
options.B: No
options.C: Only if paid after customs clearance
options.D: Only for domestic shipments

questions.explanation: Transport costs may be included under the Agreement depending on national option and applicable rules.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs reject the declared price just because it is lower than prevailing market prices?

options.A: Yes, whenever the goods are imported in bulk
options.B: Yes, if the goods are sold under discount

options.D: No, that alone is not a valid reason to reject transaction value

questions.explanation: A low price alone is insufficient to reject transaction value.

questions.question: Under Article 15, are members of the same family considered related parties?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes
options.C: Only if they own a bank together
options.D: Only if they are customs agents

questions.explanation: Family members are included among related persons.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does the WTO Valuation Committee mainly handle?

options.A: Issuing HS codes
options.B: Day-to-day cargo release at ports
options.C: Setting exchange rates for all Members
options.D: Policy issues and implementation issues

questions.explanation: The WTO Committee focuses on policy and implementation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one type of addition listed in Article 8.

options.A: Domestic taxes in the importing country
options.B: Interest charges only
options.C: Commissions and brokerage fees
options.D: Post-import advertising expenses only

questions.explanation: Commissions and brokerage can be added when conditions are met.

questions.question: What is Method 4?

options.A: Deductive value
options.B: Computed value
options.C: Transaction value
options.D: Fall-back method

questions.explanation: Method 4 is deductive value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why are exporters concerned about unfair customs valuation?

options.A: Because it prevents transport by sea
options.B: Because inflated customs values can undermine negotiated tariff reductions and market access benefits
options.C: Because it removes all need for certificates of origin
options.D: Because it eliminates all customs formalities

questions.explanation: Inflated customs values can nullify negotiated market-access gains.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How many authorized customs valuation methods are recognized under the Agreement?

options.A: Five
options.B: Seven
options.C: Six
options.D: Four

questions.explanation: The Agreement recognizes six methods.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs suspects the declared value may be inaccurate. According to the Agreement, what should happen before rejecting transaction value?

options.A: Customs should reject the value without explanation
options.B: Customs should request more information, explain its doubts, allow the importer to respond, and communicate any rejection in writing
options.C: Customs should immediately impose a reference price
options.D: Customs should automatically use the higher of two values

questions.explanation: The reasonable-doubts process requires procedural fairness.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can transaction value be used if part of the proceeds of subsequent resale revert directly or indirectly to the seller under Article 1.1(c)?

options.A: No, unless the Agreement’s requirements are satisfied through the relevant adjustment mechanism where applicable
options.B: Yes, if customs prefers Method 1
options.C: Only if the goods are exported again
options.D: Yes, resale proceeds never matter

questions.explanation: Proceeds reverting to seller raise a Method 1 issue unless properly addressed.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What was one major weakness of the Tokyo Round Valuation Code before the Uruguay Round?

options.A: It had no valuation methods at all
options.B: It prohibited transaction value
options.C: It applied only to sea freight imports
options.D: Less than one-third of the GATT contracting parties had signed it

questions.explanation: Limited participation was a key weakness.

questions.question: What two committees are established under Article 18?

options.A: Only one committee at the WTO Secretariat
options.B: The WTO Valuation Committee in Geneva and the Technical Committee on Customs Valuation at the WCO in Brussels
options.C: The Dispute Settlement Body and Council for Trade in Services
options.D: The SPS Committee and TBT Committee

questions.explanation: Article 18 establishes these two bodies.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What broad problem do customs valuation rules try to prevent?

options.A: Publication of customs legislation
options.B: Legitimate market discounts
options.C: Use of transaction value as a primary method
options.D: Arbitrary, fictitious, or inappropriate customs values

questions.explanation: The Agreement seeks to prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can international transport costs be an Article 8 addition?

options.A: Only if paid after customs clearance
options.B: Only for domestic shipments
options.C: Yes
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Transport costs may be included under the Agreement depending on national option and applicable rules.

questions.question: Must the seller be located in the exporting country for transaction value to apply?

options.A: No, the seller can be located in any country, including the importing country
options.B: Only if the buyer requests it
options.C: Yes, unless the goods move by air
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: Seller location is not determinative.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Must the order of valuation methods be followed?

options.A: No, customs can choose any method freely
options.B: No, the importer may skip directly to Method 6
options.C: Yes, the order must be followed
options.D: Only Methods 1 to 3 have to be followed

questions.explanation: The Agreement requires sequential use of the methods.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A WTO Member updates its national customs valuation law and wants the WTO Committee to understand how it implements the Agreement. What is the main transparency mechanism?

options.A: Notification of its customs valuation legislation under Article 22.1 and related transparency procedures
options.B: Using minimum values pending review
options.C: Relying only on domestic publication with no WTO transparency
options.D: Avoiding all notifications until dispute settlement occurs

questions.explanation: Legislation notifications are central to transparency.

questions.question: A buyer and seller are related because both are controlled by the same third company. Customs is considering whether to reject the declared value. What is the central legal question?

options.A: Whether the importer has appeal rights
options.B: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.C: Whether the parties used sea transport
options.D: Whether the goods are dutiable at an ad valorem rate

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone is not enough; the key question is price influence.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are arbitrary and fictitious values allowed under the Agreement?

options.A: Yes, when customs suspects fraud
options.B: Yes, in emergency situations
options.C: Yes, if identical goods cannot be found
options.D: No, they are expressly prohibited

questions.explanation: Arbitrary or fictitious values are not permitted.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article contains definitions?

options.A: Article 15
options.B: Article 13
options.C: Article 12
options.D: Article 16

questions.explanation: Definitions are in Article 15.

questions.question: Which article provides for a right of appeal?

options.A: Article 10
options.B: Article 11
options.C: Article 15
options.D: Article 12

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 4?

options.A: Transaction value
options.B: Computed value
options.C: Fall-back method
options.D: Deductive value

questions.explanation: Method 4 is deductive value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the main question customs must ask when buyer and seller are related?

options.A: Whether the goods are identical to domestic goods
options.B: Whether the buyer has a bank loan
options.C: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.D: Whether the goods are shipped by sea

options.A: Yes, discounts do not automatically disqualify the value

options.D: Only cash discounts are allowed

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which GATT article deals with freedom of transit?

options.A: Article IX
options.B: Article X
options.C: Article VII
options.D: Article V

questions.explanation: Article V deals with freedom of transit.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Under Article 15, are members of the same family considered related parties?

options.A: Only if they are customs agents

options.C: Yes

questions.explanation: Family members are included among related persons.

options.A: Annex III only

options.C: Articles 9 to 12
options.D: Articles 18 to 24

questions.explanation: The six methods are found in Articles 1 through 8.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can customs make an Article 8 adjustment if it cannot quantify the relevant cost?

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can international transport costs be an Article 8 addition?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes
options.C: Only if paid after customs clearance
options.D: Only for domestic shipments

questions.explanation: Transport costs may be included under the Agreement depending on national option and applicable rules.

questions.question: A WTO Member updates its national customs valuation law and wants the WTO Committee to understand how it implements the Agreement. What is the main transparency mechanism?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why does Article 20 provide special and differential treatment?

options.A: Because developed countries refused transaction value

questions.question: What is Method 5?

options.A: Fall-back method
options.B: Deductive value
options.C: Computed value
options.D: Transaction value of similar goods

questions.explanation: Method 5 is computed value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are arbitrary and fictitious values allowed under the Agreement?

options.A: Yes, when customs suspects fraud
options.B: No, they are expressly prohibited
options.C: Yes, if identical goods cannot be found
options.D: Yes, in emergency situations

questions.explanation: Arbitrary or fictitious values are not permitted.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which GATT article deals with publication and administration of trade regulations?

options.A: Article VII
options.B: Article VIII
options.C: Article V
options.D: Article X

questions.explanation: Article X covers publication and administration of trade regulations.

questions.question: Must the order of valuation methods be followed?

options.A: No, customs can choose any method freely
options.B: Only Methods 1 to 3 have to be followed
options.C: Yes, the order must be followed
options.D: No, the importer may skip directly to Method 6

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why does Article 20 provide special and differential treatment?

options.A: Because developed countries refused transaction value
options.B: Because valuation rules apply only to agriculture
options.C: Because all Members must be permanently exempt from valuation rules
options.D: Because developing country Members might face difficulties implementing all provisions when the WTO was created

questions.explanation: Article 20 addresses developing-country implementation difficulties.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs make an Article 8 adjustment if it cannot quantify the relevant cost?

options.A: Yes, customs may estimate it freely
options.B: No
options.C: Yes, if goods are subject to ad valorem duty
options.D: Yes, if the importer is related to the seller

questions.explanation: Unquantifiable additions cannot be made.

questions.question: What should customs consider first when examining the circumstances of sale between related parties?

options.A: How the buyer and seller organize their commercial relations
options.B: Only the HS classification
options.C: Only the importer’s transport mode
options.D: Only the customs duty rate

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the key question when using test values in related-party valuation?

questions.question: What four core elements make up transaction value under Article 1.1?

options.A: List price, insurance, profit, and exchange control charges
options.B: Any customs-selected benchmark plus margins
options.C: Price actually paid or payable, for the goods, when sold for export, plus permitted adjustments

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the last method in the hierarchy?

options.A: Computed value
options.B: Domestic selling price
options.C: Transaction value of similar goods
options.D: The fall-back method under Article 7

questions.explanation: Method 6 is the fall-back method.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article deals with publication?

options.A: Article 12
options.B: Article 16
options.C: Article 10
options.D: Article 13

questions.explanation: Publication is governed by Article 12.

questions.question: Are buyer and seller related if one is the employer of the other?

options.A: Only if there is a discount
options.B: No
options.C: Yes
options.D: Only in computed value cases

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which article deals with responsibilities of the WTO and WCO Secretariats?

options.A: Article 21
options.B: Article 23
options.C: Article 22
options.D: Article 24

questions.explanation: Article 24 allocates secretariat responsibilities.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article deals with obligations regarding national legislation?

options.A: Article 23
options.B: Article 22
options.C: Article 24
options.D: Article 21

questions.explanation: Article 22 concerns national legislation.

questions.question: Can customs value be based on the price of goods for export to a country other than the country of importation?

options.A: Yes, whenever the importer requests it

options.D: How the buyer and seller organize their commercial relations

options.D: Only if there is no invoice

options.D: No

options.D: Article 10

questions.question: Scenario variant: A shipment is sold for export to the importing country, the invoice shows a lower-than-average price because of a volume discount, and customs has no other evidence of manipulation. What is the best starting method?
