options.B: Only volume discounts are allowed
options.C: Yes, discounts do not automatically disqualify the value
options.D: No, all discounts invalidate Method 1

questions.explanation: Discounted sales can still qualify.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are legally recognized partners in business considered related parties?

options.A: Yes
options.B: Only if they own 50 percent of stock
options.C: No
options.D: Only if they import identical goods

questions.explanation: Business partners are related parties.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article contains definitions?

options.A: Article 12
options.B: Article 13
options.C: Article 16
options.D: Article 15

questions.explanation: Definitions are in Article 15.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs cannot use Methods 1 to 5. An officer proposes inventing a fixed benchmark value unrelated to the Agreement. Is that allowed under Method 6?

options.A: Yes; Method 6 allows any value customs prefers
options.B: Yes; if the importer is a related party
options.C: No; even the fall-back method cannot use arbitrary or fictitious values
options.D: Yes; if the benchmark is higher than invoice price

questions.explanation: The fall-back method remains constrained by the Agreement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the key question when using test values in related-party valuation?

options.A: Whether the test value is always higher than the import price
options.B: Whether the test values closely approximate the import price
options.C: Whether the test value is arbitrary
options.D: Whether the test value comes from domestic sales

questions.explanation: The comparison is based on close approximation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What was one major weakness of the Tokyo Round Valuation Code before the Uruguay Round?

options.A: It prohibited transaction value
options.B: It applied only to sea freight imports
options.C: It had no valuation methods at all
options.D: Less than one-third of the GATT contracting parties had signed it

questions.explanation: Limited participation was a key weakness.

questions.question: Can customs value be based on the selling price of domestically produced goods?

options.A: No, that is prohibited under Article 7
options.B: Yes, if the importer agrees
options.C: Yes, when identical imports are unavailable
options.D: Yes, if that price is higher

questions.explanation: Domestic selling price is a prohibited basis.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can a discounted sale still qualify for transaction value?

options.A: No, all discounts invalidate Method 1
options.B: Yes, discounts do not automatically disqualify the value
options.C: Only cash discounts are allowed
options.D: Only volume discounts are allowed

questions.explanation: Discounted sales can still qualify.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which GATT article deals with fees and formalities connected with importation and exportation?

options.A: Article V
options.B: Article IX
options.C: Article VII
options.D: Article VIII

questions.explanation: Article VIII covers fees and formalities.

questions.question: Can customs value be based on the higher of two alternative values?

options.A: No, that is prohibited
options.B: Yes, if customs has doubts
options.C: Yes, because it protects revenue
options.D: Yes, but only for related parties

questions.explanation: The higher-of-two-values approach is prohibited.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which GATT article deals with publication and administration of trade regulations?

options.A: Article VII
options.B: Article V
options.C: Article X
options.D: Article VIII

questions.explanation: Article X covers publication and administration of trade regulations.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the last method in the hierarchy?

options.A: Domestic selling price
options.B: The fall-back method under Article 7
options.C: Transaction value of similar goods
options.D: Computed value

questions.explanation: Method 6 is the fall-back method.

questions.question: Are officers or directors of one another’s businesses related parties?

options.A: No
options.B: Only in Annex III situations
options.C: Only for WTO accession cases
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Common officer/director connections can establish relatedness.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An officer says: 'I will use the domestic selling price of similar locally made goods because that will produce a higher customs value.' Which rule does this violate?

options.A: The developing-country transition rule
options.B: The rule on publication only
options.C: The prohibition on using the domestic selling price of domestically produced goods as customs value
options.D: The right of appeal

questions.explanation: Domestic prices of national-origin goods are prohibited valuation bases.

questions.question: A shipment is sold for export to the importing country, the invoice shows a lower-than-average price because of a volume discount, and customs has no other evidence of manipulation. What is the best starting method?

options.A: Transaction value under Method 1
options.B: Immediate use of minimum values
options.C: Automatic rejection because the price is low
options.D: Deductive value under Method 4

questions.explanation: A low price or discount alone does not disqualify Method 1.

questions.question: What does Annex III contain?

options.A: Only WTO accession schedules
options.B: Provisions relating to developing countries
options.C: Only confidentiality rules
options.D: The six methods of valuation

questions.explanation: Annex III covers developing-country provisions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which articles contain the valuation methods?

options.A: Articles 1 to 8
options.B: Articles 9 to 12
options.C: Annex III only
options.D: Articles 18 to 24

questions.explanation: The six methods are found in Articles 1 through 8.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 6?

options.A: Minimum value method
options.B: The domestic price method
options.C: The fall-back method
options.D: The highest-of-two-values method

questions.explanation: Method 6 is the fall-back method.

questions.question: An importer disagrees with a customs valuation decision and wants an official avenue to challenge it. Which safeguard in the Agreement is most directly relevant?

options.A: The rule on currency conversion under Article 9
options.B: The rule on carrier media decisions
options.C: The annex on developing countries
options.D: The right of appeal under Article 11

questions.explanation: Article 11 provides the appeal right.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The buyer supplied molds free of charge to the foreign producer for use in manufacturing the imported goods, and that value is not already in the invoice price. Which concept is most relevant?

options.A: A prohibited minimum value
options.B: A domestic price benchmark
options.C: An assist that may need to be added under Article 8 if objectively quantifiable
options.D: A post-importation cost that must be excluded

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production inputs can be assists under Article 8.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one type of addition listed in Article 8.

options.A: Domestic taxes in the importing country
options.B: Interest charges only
options.C: Post-import advertising expenses only
options.D: Commissions and brokerage fees

questions.explanation: Commissions and brokerage can be added when conditions are met.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A WTO Member updates its national customs valuation law and wants the WTO Committee to understand how it implements the Agreement. What is the main transparency mechanism?

options.A: Notification of its customs valuation legislation under Article 22.1 and related transparency procedures
options.B: Relying only on domestic publication with no WTO transparency
options.C: Avoiding all notifications until dispute settlement occurs
options.D: Using minimum values pending review

questions.explanation: Legislation notifications are central to transparency.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why does the relationship between buyer and seller matter in customs valuation?

options.A: Because relationship alone makes the value invalid
options.B: Because the relationship may or may not have influenced the price
options.C: Because customs always ignores related-party sales
options.D: Because related parties are always forbidden to trade

questions.explanation: The central question is whether the price was influenced.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is one proof that the relationship did not influence the price?

options.A: The goods are subject to ad valorem duty
options.B: The seller is overseas
options.C: The price is consistent with normal industry practice
options.D: The price is always the highest in the market

questions.explanation: Normal industry practice can indicate no influence.

questions.question: What are the two decisions mentioned as requiring notifications relating to interpretation of the Agreement?

options.A: The Decision on the Treatment of Interest Charges and the Decision on Carrier Media
options.B: The Decision on Tariff Bindings and the Decision on Transit
options.C: The Decision on Domestic Industry Prices and the Decision on Minimum Values
options.D: The Decision on SPS Measures and the Decision on Subsidies

questions.explanation: These two implementation-related decisions require notifications.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why do notification requirements exist under Article 22.1?

options.A: To replace dispute settlement
options.B: To provide transparency to the WTO Committee on each Member’s implementation of the Agreement
options.C: To remove the need for national legislation
options.D: To set tariff bindings automatically

questions.explanation: Notifications support transparency and review.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name another type of addition listed in Article 8.

options.A: Importer’s internal accounting costs
options.B: Domestic retail margin
options.C: General company dividends
options.D: Containers and packaging

questions.explanation: Containers and packaging are possible additions.

questions.question: What is a third proof that the relationship did not influence the price?

options.A: The price is stated in domestic currency
options.B: The seller issued a proforma invoice
options.C: The price allows recovery of costs plus an average profit
options.D: The price always includes customs duty

questions.explanation: Cost recovery plus average profit can support the price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Part IV of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Importer rights only
options.B: The Technical Committee’s day-to-day rulings
options.C: The six valuation methods
options.D: Final provisions in Articles 21 to 24

questions.explanation: Part IV contains the final provisions.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer pays financing charges under a loan arrangement connected to the import, but those interest charges meet the required conditions for exclusion. What does the Agreement allow?

options.A: Interest must be replaced by a minimum customs value
options.B: Interest charges may be excluded from customs value in the appropriate circumstances
options.C: All financing-related amounts must always be added
options.D: Customs must use the highest of two alternative values

questions.explanation: Interest charges can be excluded where the conditions are satisfied.

questions.question: What did GATT 1947 say customs value should not be based on?

options.A: The value of merchandise of national origin or arbitrary or fictitious values
options.B: A sale for export to the country of importation
options.C: Objective and quantifiable data
options.D: The transaction value of imported goods

questions.explanation: GATT 1947 prohibited customs value based on domestic merchandise or arbitrary/fictitious values.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What kind of transfer must the sale involve for it to qualify under transaction value?

options.A: A purely domestic sale
options.B: A movement between two warehouses in one country
options.C: Only a temporary stock movement
options.D: An international transfer of goods

questions.explanation: The sale must be linked to export to the country of importation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can transaction value be used if part of the proceeds of subsequent resale revert directly or indirectly to the seller under Article 1.1(c)?

options.A: Only if the goods are exported again
options.B: Yes, resale proceeds never matter
options.C: No, unless the Agreement’s requirements are satisfied through the relevant adjustment mechanism where applicable
options.D: Yes, if customs prefers Method 1

questions.explanation: Proceeds reverting to seller raise a Method 1 issue unless properly addressed.

questions.question: Why do notification requirements exist under Article 22.1?

options.A: To provide transparency to the WTO Committee on each Member’s implementation of the Agreement
options.B: To remove the need for national legislation
options.C: To replace dispute settlement
options.D: To set tariff bindings automatically

questions.explanation: Notifications support transparency and review.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can customs value be based on cost of production other than computed value?

options.A: Yes, if domestic law prefers it
options.B: No, that is prohibited
options.C: Yes, if the seller is related
options.D: Yes, whenever customs suspects undervaluation

questions.explanation: Only the computed value method can use production-cost concepts in the lawful hierarchy.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs reject the declared price just because it is lower than prevailing market prices?

options.A: No, that alone is not a valid reason to reject transaction value
options.B: Yes, low prices always invalidate Method 1
options.C: Yes, whenever the goods are imported in bulk
options.D: Yes, if the goods are sold under discount

questions.explanation: A low price alone is insufficient to reject transaction value.

questions.question: What voting stock threshold helps establish relatedness through common ownership or control?

options.A: Fifty percent
options.B: Ten percent
options.C: One percent
options.D: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person

questions.explanation: The Agreement uses the five-percent threshold in this category.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which GATT article deals with freedom of transit?

options.A: Article IX
options.B: Article V
options.C: Article VII
options.D: Article X

questions.explanation: Article V deals with freedom of transit.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the main question customs must ask when buyer and seller are related?

options.A: Whether the goods are shipped by sea
options.B: Whether the buyer has a bank loan
options.C: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.D: Whether the goods are identical to domestic goods

questions.explanation: This is the key test in related-party cases.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs lawfully rejects Method 1 and cannot apply Method 2 because no qualifying identical-goods comparison exists. What comes next in the hierarchy?

options.A: Method 3: transaction value of similar goods
options.B: Method 5: computed value
options.C: Method 6: fall-back
options.D: Return to Method 1 with a higher estimate

questions.explanation: The hierarchy moves from identical goods to similar goods.

questions.question: An importing country binds tariffs at low ad valorem rates, but customs officers start inflating customs values to raise more duty. Which key problem identified in the Agreement does this illustrate?

options.A: The lawful use of minimum values
options.B: Article 10 confidentiality breaches
options.C: Transaction value always prevents under-collection only
options.D: Inflated customs values can nullify tariff concessions and undermine market access

questions.explanation: The Agreement exists in part to prevent inflated values from defeating tariff concessions.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Annex III contain?

options.A: Only WTO accession schedules
options.B: The six methods of valuation
options.C: Provisions relating to developing countries
options.D: Only confidentiality rules

questions.explanation: Annex III covers developing-country provisions.

questions.question: Name another type of addition listed in Article 8.

options.A: General company dividends
options.B: Containers and packaging
options.C: Domestic retail margin
options.D: Importer’s internal accounting costs

questions.explanation: Containers and packaging are possible additions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can transaction value be rejected if customs has unresolved reasonable doubts after following the proper process?

options.A: Only if the goods came by sea
options.B: No, if the importer is related to the seller
options.C: Yes
options.D: No, transaction value can never be rejected

questions.explanation: After the required procedure, transaction value may be rejected if doubts remain unresolved.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which general principle appears in the preamble of the Agreement?

options.A: Valuation should be fair, uniform, and neutral and based on simple and equitable criteria consistent with commercial practice
options.B: Valuation should maximize revenue regardless of trade effects
options.C: Valuation should always be based on domestic market prices
options.D: Valuation should always use minimum prices

questions.explanation: These are core preambular principles.

tests.title: WTO Agreement on Customs Valuation - Main Test 7
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: An importing country binds tariffs at low ad valorem rates, but customs officers start inflating customs values to raise more duty. Which key problem identified in the Agreement does this illustrate?

options.A: Article 10 confidentiality breaches
options.B: The lawful use of minimum values
options.C: Transaction value always prevents under-collection only
options.D: Inflated customs values can nullify tariff concessions and undermine market access

questions.explanation: The Agreement exists in part to prevent inflated values from defeating tariff concessions.

questions.question: Which GATT article deals specifically with valuation for customs purposes?

options.A: Article X
options.B: Article VIII
options.C: Article VII
options.D: Article V

questions.explanation: Article VII is the main GATT provision on customs valuation.

questions.question: There is no actual sale for export to the country of importation, only a transfer of goods between branches of the same company without sale. What follows?

options.A: Customs must use domestic market price
options.B: Method 1 transaction value cannot be used because there is no sale
options.C: Customs must use minimum values
options.D: Method 1 still applies automatically

questions.explanation: A sale is required for Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What broad problem do customs valuation rules try to prevent?

options.A: Arbitrary, fictitious, or inappropriate customs values
options.B: Use of transaction value as a primary method
options.C: Legitimate market discounts
options.D: Publication of customs legislation

questions.explanation: The Agreement seeks to prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values.

questions.question: What other instruments form part of the current customs valuation rules besides the Agreement itself?

options.A: Only domestic court judgments
options.B: Only tariff schedules
options.C: Ministerial decisions and Committee decisions, including decisions on reasonable doubts, minimum values and sole agents, and G/VAL/5
options.D: Only the Harmonized System Explanatory Notes

questions.explanation: Current valuation rules include relevant ministerial and committee decisions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Annex I contain?

options.A: Only definitions of related parties
options.B: Developing-country transition rules only
options.C: The WTO Committee rules of procedure
options.D: Interpretative Notes

questions.explanation: Annex I contains interpretative notes.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Must the order of valuation methods be followed?

options.A: Only Methods 1 to 3 have to be followed
options.B: Yes, the order must be followed
options.C: No, the importer may skip directly to Method 6
options.D: No, customs can choose any method freely

questions.explanation: The Agreement requires sequential use of the methods.

questions.question: Which articles contain the valuation methods?

options.A: Articles 1 to 8
options.B: Annex III only
options.C: Articles 18 to 24
options.D: Articles 9 to 12

questions.explanation: The six methods are found in Articles 1 through 8.

questions.question: A shipment is sold for export to the importing country, the invoice shows a lower-than-average price because of a volume discount, and customs has no other evidence of manipulation. What is the best starting method?

options.A: Deductive value under Method 4
options.B: Transaction value under Method 1
options.C: Immediate use of minimum values
options.D: Automatic rejection because the price is low

questions.explanation: A low price or discount alone does not disqualify Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What did GATT 1947 say customs value should not be based on?

options.A: Objective and quantifiable data
options.B: A sale for export to the country of importation
options.C: The transaction value of imported goods
options.D: The value of merchandise of national origin or arbitrary or fictitious values

questions.explanation: GATT 1947 prohibited customs value based on domestic merchandise or arbitrary/fictitious values.

questions.question: Which article deals with responsibilities of the WTO and WCO Secretariats?

options.A: Article 21
options.B: Article 22
options.C: Article 24
options.D: Article 23

questions.explanation: Article 24 allocates secretariat responsibilities.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs reviews a related-party sale and finds the price matches sales to unrelated buyers for equivalent quantities and commercial conditions. What is the likely implication?

options.A: The related-party relationship may be treated as not having influenced the price, allowing transaction value
options.B: Customs must automatically use computed value
options.C: The importer loses the right of appeal
options.D: The value must still be rejected because the parties are related

questions.explanation: Comparable prices to unrelated buyers can support Method 1.

questions.question: An importer pays financing charges under a loan arrangement connected to the import, but those interest charges meet the required conditions for exclusion. What does the Agreement allow?

options.A: Customs must use the highest of two alternative values
options.B: All financing-related amounts must always be added
options.C: Interest must be replaced by a minimum customs value
options.D: Interest charges may be excluded from customs value in the appropriate circumstances

questions.explanation: Interest charges can be excluded where the conditions are satisfied.

questions.question: Scenario variant: There is no actual sale for export to the country of importation, only a transfer of goods between branches of the same company without sale. What follows?

options.A: Method 1 transaction value cannot be used because there is no sale
options.B: Method 1 still applies automatically
options.C: Customs must use minimum values
options.D: Customs must use domestic market price

questions.explanation: A sale is required for Method 1.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Part I of the Customs Valuation Agreement cover?

options.A: Administration and dispute settlement only
options.B: Rules on customs valuation in Articles 1 to 17
options.C: Special and differential treatment only
options.D: Final provisions only

questions.explanation: Part I contains the valuation rules.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A shipment is sold for export to the importing country, the invoice shows a lower-than-average price because of a volume discount, and customs has no other evidence of manipulation. What is the best starting method?

options.A: Automatic rejection because the price is low
options.B: Deductive value under Method 4
options.C: Transaction value under Method 1
options.D: Immediate use of minimum values

questions.explanation: A low price or discount alone does not disqualify Method 1.

questions.question: What broad problem do customs valuation rules try to prevent?

options.A: Use of transaction value as a primary method
options.B: Arbitrary, fictitious, or inappropriate customs values
options.C: Publication of customs legislation
options.D: Legitimate market discounts

questions.explanation: The Agreement seeks to prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs lawfully rejects Method 1 and cannot apply Method 2 because no qualifying identical-goods comparison exists. What comes next in the hierarchy?

options.A: Method 5: computed value
options.B: Return to Method 1 with a higher estimate
options.C: Method 6: fall-back
options.D: Method 3: transaction value of similar goods

questions.explanation: The hierarchy moves from identical goods to similar goods.

questions.question: Customs suspects the declared value may be inaccurate. According to the Agreement, what should happen before rejecting transaction value?

options.A: Customs should request more information, explain its doubts, allow the importer to respond, and communicate any rejection in writing
options.B: Customs should automatically use the higher of two values
options.C: Customs should immediately impose a reference price
options.D: Customs should reject the value without explanation

questions.explanation: The reasonable-doubts process requires procedural fairness.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs cannot use Methods 1 to 5. An officer proposes inventing a fixed benchmark value unrelated to the Agreement. Is that allowed under Method 6?

options.A: Yes; if the benchmark is higher than invoice price
options.B: Yes; if the importer is a related party
options.C: No; even the fall-back method cannot use arbitrary or fictitious values
options.D: Yes; Method 6 allows any value customs prefers

questions.explanation: The fall-back method remains constrained by the Agreement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can customs reject the declared price just because it is lower than prices for identical goods in other transactions?

options.A: No, that alone is not enough to reject transaction value
options.B: Yes, customs must always use the higher price
options.C: Yes, but only for unrelated parties
options.D: Yes, identical goods prices automatically override the importer’s price

questions.explanation: Comparison with other prices alone does not justify rejection.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs value be based on the domestic market price of the country of exportation?

options.A: Yes, if those prices are publicly available
options.B: Yes, if the goods are discounted
options.C: Yes, when transport costs are unclear
options.D: No, that is prohibited

questions.explanation: Export-country domestic prices are prohibited.

questions.question: What should customs consider first when examining the circumstances of sale between related parties?

options.A: Only the HS classification
options.B: Only the importer’s transport mode
options.C: How the buyer and seller organize their commercial relations
options.D: Only the customs duty rate

questions.explanation: Commercial organization of the relationship is one factor.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 2?

options.A: Transaction value
options.B: Transaction value of identical goods
options.C: Computed value
options.D: Transaction value of similar goods

questions.explanation: Method 2 is identical goods.

questions.question: A shipment is sold for export to the importing country, the invoice shows a lower-than-average price because of a volume discount, and customs has no other evidence of manipulation. What is the best starting method?

options.A: Automatic rejection because the price is low
options.B: Immediate use of minimum values
options.C: Transaction value under Method 1
options.D: Deductive value under Method 4

questions.explanation: A low price or discount alone does not disqualify Method 1.

questions.question: What kinds of payments are included in the total payment for transaction value purposes?

options.A: Only payments made after importation
options.B: All payments the buyer makes to, or for the benefit of, the seller, whether directly or indirectly, including those required as conditions of sale
options.C: Only customs duties and inland taxes
options.D: Only visible invoice payments

questions.explanation: The concept includes direct and indirect payments benefiting the seller.

questions.question: Customs lawfully rejects Method 1 and cannot apply Method 2 because no qualifying identical-goods comparison exists. What comes next in the hierarchy?

options.A: Method 3: transaction value of similar goods
options.B: Method 6: fall-back
options.C: Method 5: computed value
options.D: Return to Method 1 with a higher estimate

questions.explanation: The hierarchy moves from identical goods to similar goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why is customs value important when duty is ad valorem?

options.A: Because ad valorem duties ignore value entirely
options.B: Because customs value matters only for statistics
options.C: Because customs value replaces tariff rates
options.D: Because the customs value is the base on which the percentage duty is calculated

questions.explanation: Ad valorem duty is calculated as a percentage of customs value.

questions.question: A buyer and seller are related because both are controlled by the same third company. Customs is considering whether to reject the declared value. What is the central legal question?

options.A: Whether the importer has appeal rights
options.B: Whether the goods are dutiable at an ad valorem rate
options.C: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.D: Whether the parties used sea transport

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone is not enough; the key question is price influence.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A country applying to join the WTO still uses official minimum customs values for many imports. What is the likely accession issue?

options.A: Minimum values are encouraged during accession
options.B: Acceding Members are exempt from valuation disciplines permanently
options.C: Reference prices replace the need for transaction value in accession talks
options.D: WTO Members generally require elimination of minimum values and full implementation of the Agreement from accession

questions.explanation: Minimum values are a common accession problem because they are prohibited.

questions.question: There is no actual sale for export to the country of importation, only a transfer of goods between branches of the same company without sale. What follows?

options.A: Method 1 transaction value cannot be used because there is no sale
options.B: Method 1 still applies automatically
options.C: Customs must use domestic market price
options.D: Customs must use minimum values

questions.explanation: A sale is required for Method 1.

questions.question: What does 'price actually paid or payable' mean in customs valuation?

options.A: Only the seller’s cost of production
options.B: Whatever amount the buyer agrees to pay the seller to obtain the goods
options.C: Only the amount stated in a public price list
options.D: Only the amount customs thinks is reasonable

questions.explanation: The concept captures the agreed payment for the goods.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 6?

options.A: Minimum value method
options.B: The domestic price method
options.C: The highest-of-two-values method
options.D: The fall-back method

questions.explanation: Method 6 is the fall-back method.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs suspects the declared value may be inaccurate. According to the Agreement, what should happen before rejecting transaction value?

options.A: Customs should reject the value without explanation
options.B: Customs should immediately impose a reference price
options.C: Customs should request more information, explain its doubts, allow the importer to respond, and communicate any rejection in writing
options.D: Customs should automatically use the higher of two values

questions.explanation: The reasonable-doubts process requires procedural fairness.

questions.question: What is another proof that the relationship did not influence the price?

options.A: The shipment moved by air
options.B: The price is the same as in sales to unrelated parties
options.C: The price is lower than every market benchmark
options.D: The importer requested a discount

questions.explanation: Comparable pricing to unrelated buyers can support acceptance.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What valuation method has primacy under the Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: The transaction value method
options.B: The domestic selling price method
options.C: The computed value method
options.D: The fall-back method

questions.explanation: Transaction value is the primary method.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs value be based on the domestic market price of the country of exportation?

options.A: Yes, if the goods are discounted
options.B: No, that is prohibited
options.C: Yes, when transport costs are unclear
options.D: Yes, if those prices are publicly available

questions.explanation: Export-country domestic prices are prohibited.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A buyer and seller are related because both are controlled by the same third company. Customs is considering whether to reject the declared value. What is the central legal question?

options.A: Whether the importer has appeal rights
options.B: Whether the parties used sea transport
options.C: Whether the goods are dutiable at an ad valorem rate
options.D: Whether the relationship influenced the price

options.A: Method 5: computed value
options.B: Method 3: transaction value of similar goods
options.C: Method 6: fall-back
options.D: Return to Method 1 with a higher estimate

questions.explanation: The hierarchy moves from identical goods to similar goods.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs reviews a related-party sale and finds the price matches sales to unrelated buyers for equivalent quantities and commercial conditions. What is the likely implication?

options.A: The related-party relationship may be treated as not having influenced the price, allowing transaction value
options.B: The value must still be rejected because the parties are related
options.C: Customs must automatically use computed value
options.D: The importer loses the right of appeal

questions.explanation: Comparable prices to unrelated buyers can support Method 1.

questions.question: An importer pays financing charges under a loan arrangement connected to the import, but those interest charges meet the required conditions for exclusion. What does the Agreement allow?

options.A: All financing-related amounts must always be added
options.B: Interest charges may be excluded from customs value in the appropriate circumstances
options.C: Interest must be replaced by a minimum customs value
options.D: Customs must use the highest of two alternative values

questions.explanation: Interest charges can be excluded where the conditions are satisfied.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A WTO Member updates its national customs valuation law and wants the WTO Committee to understand how it implements the Agreement. What is the main transparency mechanism?

options.A: Using minimum values pending review
options.B: Avoiding all notifications until dispute settlement occurs
options.C: Relying only on domestic publication with no WTO transparency
options.D: Notification of its customs valuation legislation under Article 22.1 and related transparency procedures

questions.explanation: Legislation notifications are central to transparency.

questions.question: Customs reviews a related-party sale and finds the price matches sales to unrelated buyers for equivalent quantities and commercial conditions. What is the likely implication?

options.A: The value must still be rejected because the parties are related
options.B: Customs must automatically use computed value
options.C: The related-party relationship may be treated as not having influenced the price, allowing transaction value
options.D: The importer loses the right of appeal

questions.explanation: Comparable prices to unrelated buyers can support Method 1.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer pays financing charges under a loan arrangement connected to the import, but those interest charges meet the required conditions for exclusion. What does the Agreement allow?

options.A: Interest must be replaced by a minimum customs value
options.B: Interest charges may be excluded from customs value in the appropriate circumstances
options.C: All financing-related amounts must always be added
options.D: Customs must use the highest of two alternative values

questions.explanation: Interest charges can be excluded where the conditions are satisfied.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the general objective of customs management and regulation under the GATT in relation to valuation?

options.A: To require all Members to use minimum values
options.B: To replace WTO rules with domestic price controls
options.C: To maximize customs barriers wherever possible
options.D: To ensure customs operations do not unnecessarily hinder international trade or offset concessions exchanged between Members

questions.explanation: The GATT framework seeks to avoid customs operations that unnecessarily hinder trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one type of addition listed in Article 8.

options.A: Commissions and brokerage fees
options.B: Domestic taxes in the importing country
options.C: Post-import advertising expenses only
options.D: Interest charges only

questions.explanation: Commissions and brokerage can be added when conditions are met.

questions.question: An officer says: 'I will use the domestic selling price of similar locally made goods because that will produce a higher customs value.' Which rule does this violate?

options.A: The right of appeal
options.B: The rule on publication only
options.C: The prohibition on using the domestic selling price of domestically produced goods as customs value
options.D: The developing-country transition rule

questions.explanation: Domestic prices of national-origin goods are prohibited valuation bases.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What should customs consider first when examining the circumstances of sale between related parties?

options.A: How the buyer and seller organize their commercial relations
options.B: Only the HS classification
options.C: Only the importer’s transport mode
options.D: Only the customs duty rate

questions.explanation: Commercial organization of the relationship is one factor.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Which article deals with publication?

options.A: Article 10
options.B: Article 16
options.C: Article 13
options.D: Article 12

questions.explanation: Publication is governed by Article 12.

questions.question: What does Part IV of the Agreement cover?

options.A: The six valuation methods
options.B: The Technical Committee’s day-to-day rulings
options.C: Final provisions in Articles 21 to 24
options.D: Importer rights only

questions.explanation: Part IV contains the final provisions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why does Article 20 provide special and differential treatment?

options.A: Because developed countries refused transaction value
options.B: Because developing country Members might face difficulties implementing all provisions when the WTO was created
options.C: Because valuation rules apply only to agriculture
options.D: Because all Members must be permanently exempt from valuation rules

questions.explanation: Article 20 addresses developing-country implementation difficulties.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A shipment is sold for export to the importing country, the invoice shows a lower-than-average price because of a volume discount, and customs has no other evidence of manipulation. What is the best starting method?

options.A: Transaction value under Method 1
options.B: Immediate use of minimum values
options.C: Deductive value under Method 4
options.D: Automatic rejection because the price is low

questions.explanation: A low price or discount alone does not disqualify Method 1.

questions.question: Which article deals with currency conversion?

options.A: Article 13
options.B: Article 11
options.C: Article 9
options.D: Article 10

questions.explanation: Article 9 addresses currency conversion.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Which article contains definitions?

options.A: Article 12
options.B: Article 15
options.C: Article 16
options.D: Article 13

questions.explanation: Definitions are in Article 15.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What was the aim of the Uruguay Round negotiations on customs valuation?

options.A: To confine the code to a small group of signatories
options.B: To abolish all valuation disciplines
options.C: To allow minimum values universally
options.D: To improve, clarify, or expand the Tokyo Round Code and make it a multilateral agreement binding on WTO Members

questions.explanation: The Uruguay Round sought a stronger, broader multilateral agreement.

questions.question: Can customs reject the declared price just because it is lower than prices for identical goods in other transactions?

options.A: Yes, identical goods prices automatically override the importer’s price
options.B: Yes, but only for unrelated parties
options.C: No, that alone is not enough to reject transaction value
options.D: Yes, customs must always use the higher price

questions.explanation: Comparison with other prices alone does not justify rejection.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Part II of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Valuation methods only
options.B: Annexes only
options.C: Special and differential treatment only
options.D: Administration, consultations, and dispute settlement in Articles 18 and 19

questions.explanation: Part II deals with institutional and dispute matters.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are 'assists' under Article 8 in general terms?

options.A: Certain goods or services supplied by the buyer for use in producing the imported goods, which may need to be added if not already included
options.B: All transport costs paid after import
options.C: Any payment made by the bank
options.D: All domestic services unrelated to production

questions.explanation: Assists are buyer-supplied inputs or services related to production.

questions.question: An officer says: 'I will use the domestic selling price of similar locally made goods because that will produce a higher customs value.' Which rule does this violate?

options.A: The developing-country transition rule
options.B: The prohibition on using the domestic selling price of domestically produced goods as customs value
options.C: The rule on publication only
options.D: The right of appeal

questions.explanation: Domestic prices of national-origin goods are prohibited valuation bases.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 5?

options.A: Deductive value
options.B: Transaction value of similar goods
options.C: Fall-back method
options.D: Computed value

questions.explanation: Method 5 is computed value.

questions.question: Customs cannot use Methods 1 to 5. An officer proposes inventing a fixed benchmark value unrelated to the Agreement. Is that allowed under Method 6?

options.A: Yes; if the benchmark is higher than invoice price
options.B: Yes; Method 6 allows any value customs prefers
options.C: No; even the fall-back method cannot use arbitrary or fictitious values
options.D: Yes; if the importer is a related party

questions.explanation: The fall-back method remains constrained by the Agreement.
